name: agent-orchestrator

name: agentic-capability

Use when user asks "should I build a skill or a command?", "what's the best way to implement this?", "should this be a subagent?", "how should I structure these skills?", "I want to build X what should I use?", "skill vs command", "how do I add this capability", "design this agentic capability", "what mechanism should I use", "agentic capability", "what should I build".
description_normalized: Design advisor for choosing the right Claude Code extension mechanism before building anything.

name: code-quality-audit
description_raw: Deep code quality audit of production source files. Think like a senior software architect reviewing code before building a major feature on top of it. Use when user says "code quality audit", "audit the code", "review production code", "is this code ready", "grade the code", "quality check", "before building on this", "code health check", "review my implementation", "is this codebase solid". Always launch in parallel with appydave:test-quality-audit — they cover different concerns and neither substitutes for the other.
description_normalized: Deep code quality audit of production source files.

name: kcommit
description_raw: Git commit workflow for Ruby gems and AppyDave projects. Only trigger when user explicitly names a k-function: kcommit, kfeat, kfix, kchore, kdocs, ktest, krefactor, kenhance, kcops. Do NOT trigger on generic phrases like "commit this", "commit with message", or "push".
description_normalized: Git commit workflow for Ruby gems and AppyDave projects.

name: remote-machines
description_raw: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH. Use when user says "run this on the mac mini", "run it on the other machine", "ssh to mac-mini", "deploy to the agent machine", "bot machine", "headless machine", "workstation machine", "send this to mac-mini-m4", "mac-mini-m2", "remote machines", "other machines", or any request involving executing something on a machine other than the current one. Also use proactively when a task would clearly benefit from running on a specific machine (e.g. a background job on the headless agent machine).
description_normalized: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH.

name: session-checkpoint
description_raw: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly. Use when user says "checkpoint", "save my progress", "checkpoint the workflow", "where am I up to", "save task state", "checkpoint before this next step", or when about to do a risky operation or context is filling up. Captures step-by-step progress through a plan or task list — NOT a session summary (use capture-context) and NOT durable knowledge (use knowledge-capture).
description_normalized: Checkpoint current task and workflow progress mid-session so it can be restored exactly.

name: alex
description_raw: Workflow Architect & Orchestration Designer — conversational, analytical, design-focused. Interviews users to understand orchestration goals, scans existing prompts and schemas to build an I/O inventory (activation Step 4), maps data flow, and generates clean workflow YAML with human checkpoints plus a companion .usage.md guide. Commands: *design, *analyze, *generate, *validate, *refine. Dependencies: 5 workflows, 3 data files, 3 skills.

name: using-git-worktrees
description_raw: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback. Detects existing isolation first, defers to native harness tools, falls back to manual git worktree with safety verification, then runs project setup and clean baseline test.
description_normalized: Workspace isolation skill that detects existing worktrees, defers to native harness tools, and falls back to git worktrees with safety checks.

name: writing-plans
description_raw: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code. Writes comprehensive implementation plans: file structure map first, then bite-sized tasks (2-5 min steps) with exact file paths, complete code, exact commands and expected output. No placeholders. Every plan includes required REQUIRED SUB-SKILL header. Ends with execution handoff choice (subagent-driven vs inline).
description_normalized: Implementation plan authoring skill that produces zero-placeholder, TDD-structured, bite-sized task plans with complete code at each step.
